title: iPhone 17 Pro - Nieopłacalny, brzydki i gruby
transcript: Ugly, thick, not scratch-resistant and not worth the price at all .  I heard these terms most often while watching every review of the IPA 17 Pro, so I wasn't very positive about this phone. But what is it like in reality?   I'll tell you a little about it in a moment.  Starting with the design, construction, build quality, and surprisingly, this is the thing that caused the most controversy in the case of the new iPhones.  A new design is one thing.  Some like it, others don't , as is usually the case in life. However, in the case of the 17 pro, the second group, i.e. people who do not like this design, seems to be much larger.  People who watched my video recorded just after the Apple conference know that I called this phone simply awful.  It may be embarrassing to admit it, but over time I simply started to like this phone.  Already after the first photos that appeared from people who actually held this phone in their hands, I came to the conclusion that okay, maybe it 's not as ugly as I thought.  When I saw him live for the first time, I also thought to myself, damn, he looks pretty good.  When I started using it myself, I came to the conclusion that this design is really cool.  I also recommend that people who, for example, would like to buy the 17 Pro, but are stopped by the thought that this phone is ugly, should see it in person, because it really makes a much better impression in person than in the photos and than it did at the conference.   I especially like it in this blue color.  I have the impression that this is probably the least popular color this year, because a lot of people are excited about this orange.  There is also a large group of people who simply rationally choose silver so that there are no visible scratches on it.  Is it prettier than previous models?  I don't know.  After a while I simply started to like it very much.  It certainly looks completely different.  I think the previous models were a bit more elegant, a bit more premium looking.  I really like the saying about the 17 Pro that it looks like an iPhone from AliExpress.  There is actually something to it when I compare it to the previous models.  However, as I said, after seeing this phone in person a few times, I simply started to like it.  The second issue is the construction of this phone, the quality of its workmanship.  As I think everyone who is into this topic knows, Apple has moved away from the titanium used in previous models and returned to aluminum, so we have a pretty strong return to the past here.  Apple supported this decision by saying that the phone would cope much better with overheating. I'll tell you a little later whether it worked out.  The result was that for the first few days of the week the internet was flooded with photos where the 17 Pro was either scratched all over, had chipped corners, or the paint was chipping after a few days of use.  I approached it with considerable distance.  I feel like people kind of forget that something like this actually happens every year.  Even when the 16 Pro was released, which was theoretically made of this much more durable titanium, there were also photos flying around where, first of all, the back of the phone was completely scratched in Apple Stores because it was placed on the charger.  In addition, there were photos where the paint was chipping off the frames.  So, as I said, I approached these photos with a bit of distance. Is aluminum generally less resistant than titanium?  Yes.  Were most of those photos that showed the 17 Pro as a completely scratched phone after a few days of use fabricated in some way ? Well, I think so too.  Personally, when I leave the house, I always carry my phone in my Cas and I also have a tempered glass screen protector on it.  The only times I have this phone without a case are when I use it at home without it.  So I'm not a good person to say whether aluminum will be significantly less durable than titanium.  On paper , of course it is.  On the other hand, I don't know many people who carry their phones every day without any protective case.  Considering that the 16 Pro wasn't super resistant to scratches or chipping paint, in my opinion, if someone carries their phone without a case or a protective glass and carries it in their pocket, sometimes with coins or sometimes with keys, whether they have the 16 Pro or the 17 Pro, the phone will be heavily scratched after a few weeks or months anyway .  It is probably the case that the 17 Pro will simply scratch faster and more severely in such a situation.  Well, unfortunately, that's how it is .  When it comes to the display, we have practically the same as the 16 Pro.  This is also the screen that all phones in the 17 line have received. There is a change here in terms of peak brightness, which has now increased from 2000 nits.  It's not some incredibly noticeable difference.   I would rather not suggest this particular thing .  For example, if you are hesitating between, for example, the 16 Pro and the 17 Pro or the 16 Pro and the 17. But overall, the screen is outstanding. Of course 120 Hz, great colors, great sharpness.  Well, in this display everything simply works as it should.  In terms of the overall experience of using the phone, it's really great here.  The phone is incredibly efficient.  We have an A19 Pro processor and 12 GB of RAM on board. Also in terms of specifications, this is a phone that should meet the expectations of even the most demanding users. Playing games, editing videos or editing photos on the phone shouldn't be a problem for him. I don't do any of these things on my phone, so I can't comment. However, I know from other tests that this phone is simply incredibly efficient.  Also, with the kind of use I use it on a daily basis, there is no way this phone would hang up.  I know I don't use its potential 100%, but I think everyone knows that it's simply one of the most efficient phones currently on the market.  In terms of use, another important issue is the overheating of this phone, or rather the lack of overheating, which was achieved thanks to the change from titanium to aluminum.  and thanks to the Vapor Chamber used in the 17 Pro , the 16 Pro liked to heat up.  This was something that many users complained about.  It bothered me a bit too .  The phone actually heated up during very simple tasks. The worst was when charging, when using the camera for a long time, and when recording videos, for example, the phone practically got hot.  In 17% of cases this problem was solved.  The phone hardly heats up at all.  Of course, for example, when you charge it and use it at the same time, it will heat up slightly.  However, this is absolutely not the level of heating of the 16 Pro.  And with that kind of normal use, this phone is possibly lukewarm most of the time, but I'd say it's pretty much always cool. Apple has done its homework on this matter too.  Apple also did its homework on the thing I personally had the biggest problem with in the 16 Pro: the battery.  And honestly, I'm surprised why there's so little talk about this battery, especially in Polish reviews. Abroad it is a little more appreciated.  This may be due to the fact that the US version of the phone does not have a physical SIM card tray .  There is only a SIM card, which makes the battery slightly larger. However, I have the standard model with a SIM card tray and the battery is truly phenomenal.  Phenomenal by today's standards, of course.  In the case of the 16 Pro, I was used to having to charge the phone almost every time during the day.  It was rare for me to get a full day of battery life.  This only happened when I really didn't use my phone much during the day.  In the case of the 17 Pro, this phone almost always lasts me a full day on battery, and when I use this phone little, which I don't use much, I suspect it will still be plenty for some people .  This phone lasts me up to a day and a half on one battery. An example from yesterday.  I unplugged the phone at 7:45 a.m. on Tuesday and with about 6 hours of screen time, it was about 5 hours and 45 minutes. Moreover, the phone was used quite intensively and I used navigation a lot for about two or three hours. I had Bluetooth headphones connected almost the entire time and was generally listening to something on them. Overall, it wasn't a light day, but the phone was a bit worn out. Anyway, as I said, the phone was unplugged at 7:45 a.m. on Tuesday and had 95% battery, because that's my set limit.  On Wednesday at 10:42 the phone still had 16% battery left and I suspect that with that 15% I could easily last until 12:00 or 1:00, even if I used the phone a bit intensively during that time .  And I assumed that it would be a day when I wouldn't use navigation, I wouldn't use Bluetooth, but generally I would use some rather undemanding processes.  Some might say it's a bold statement, but in my opinion the 17 Pro can last 2 days on a single battery.  That is, I'm talking about disconnecting the phone from the charger, for example, on Monday morning and only having to connect it on Tuesday evening.   In my opinion, this is achievable on this phone .  16 Pro, if I managed to last from morning to evening, I was really very surprised that something like that was achieved.  I know a lot of people don't mention that this battery has been improved so much. also the increase in terms of mamper hour is theoretically not so large as to make such a difference. However, in practice, in my case it looks like this and I don't know, maybe I just got some examples, my 16 Pro held the screen exceptionally poorly and my 17 Pro holds it exceptionally well.  However, my conclusions are that after using the 16 Pro for a few months and after using the 17 Pro for a few weeks, the 17 Pro has made tremendous progress when it comes to battery life.  The charging speed has also been improved and the 17 Pro can be charged to 50% in about 20 minutes. So, in fact, charging this phone for 20 minutes will give us a whole day of low-intensity battery life.  When it comes to the camera, this year the main factor determining whether you choose the basic 17 or the iPhone 17 Pro.  The camera is really great, but it's largely the same as the 16 Pro, as the main lens and the wide-angle lens are exactly the same .  The only upgrade we have is the telephoto lens, in which the number of megapixels has increased from 12 to 48 and we have a greater zoom.  Here 's the thing, I also don't know why many people repeated the incorrect information that it is an eight-times optical zoom.  Apple has named it quite specifically, very cleverly, as it stopped at Apple, that it is an eight-times zoom with the quality of an optical zoom.  And in reality, it looks like this: we have a four-times optical zoom here , and the eight-times zoom is simply a dot made from this four-times zoom, digitally enhanced. If the lighting conditions are good, this 8x zoom is indeed very similar to the 4x zoom and I think that few eyes will notice any differences here.  These 8x zoom photos are very good, very sharp.  Things are a bit worse in low light conditions.  However, it is indeed a zoom that resembles an optical zoom and is of very good quality.  What is new is the front camera, which is also the same in all models.  This is the front camera, where, first of all, in terms of megapixels, we have an increase here again from 12 to 24 megapixels. However, an interesting change has been introduced , namely a square sensor that allows you to take photos with the front camera both vertically and horizontally, while holding the phone normally vertically.  Of course, take photos and record videos.  If someone loves taking selfies with their phone, they will definitely be delighted with this camera.  It's really great quality and it's really cool that you can take photos both horizontally and vertically, holding the phone normally vertically. To sum up, the 17 Pro is absolutely not a revolution.  Apart from this new design and casing change, there have been really minimal changes compared to the 16 Pro.  However, I am personally surprised that many reviewers who reviewed previous models and commented on the jumps from the 14 Pro to the 15 Pro and from the 15 Pro to the 16 Pro, somehow it didn't bother them back then , and now suddenly small changes bother them.  And of course, there is something a bit sad about the fact that we no longer eagerly await the launch of a new phone, because we are somewhat aware that nothing revolutionary will probably happen there.  And this year, the topic is being discussed a lot, that this basic 17, compared to the basic 16, is outstanding, unbelievably good.  The only profitable phone.  I just have the impression that not enough attention is paid to the fact that in the basic 16 there were an incredible number of areas that could be improved, because let's face it, these basic iPhone models compared to the competition or to the Pro models from Apple were simply backward.  I will also be recording a video at 5:00 p.m., so I will devote more time to her. My point is simply that the 17 Pro is quite underrated, because many elements have not received any major upgrades compared to the 16 Pro. In my opinion, the 17 PR is a really great phone, mainly because of the battery, which has been improved to a degree that is very surprising to me , because it is, after all, great.  And this was the thing that annoyed me the most about the 16 Pro .  The overheating problem has been eliminated, which is also definitely a plus, because in the 16 Pro it was really annoying. The telephoto lens has been improved.  Here we have the possibility of a larger zoom, this time digitally optical.  Okay, someone might complain that this isn't full optical zoom.  So it doesn't really add anything.  But the quality of this zoom is actually the same as optical zoom.  And okay, these aren't revolutionary changes.  Unless you are a fanatic or want to record YouTube videos about it, switching from the 16 Pro to the 17 Pro is absolutely not worth it.  The 17 Pro, on the other hand, is a truly great phone that addresses many of the areas that should have been improved, given how the 16 Pro performs.  So did this phone bring something amazing, something absolutely revolutionary? NO.  But has Apple done its homework and improved the areas that should be improved?  Yes. So to sum up, who is the 17 Pro for?  For people who demand maximum performance from their phone, for people who care about a great battery life in their phone, for people who care about the best possible camera in their phone, and for people who were annoyed by overheating in the 16 Pro, for example .
